book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
title: Session V.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Paschasinus and Lucentius the most reverend bishops and Boniface a presbyter, vicars of the Apostolic See of Rome, said: If they do not agree to the letter of that apostolic and blessed man, Pope Leo, give directions that we be given our letters of dismission, and let a synod be held there [i.e. in the West].

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The most reverend bishops cried out: Many years to the Emperor!

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Either let the definition [i.e. the one presented at this session] stand or we go.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Cecropius, the most reverend bishop of Sebastopol, said: We ask that the definition be read again and that those who dissent from it, and will not sign, may go about their business; for we give our consent to these things which have been so beautifully drafted, and make no criticisms.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The most blessed bishops of Illyria said: Let those who contradict be made manifest.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Those who contradict, let them go to Rome.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The most magnificent and most glorious judges said: Dioscorus acknowledged that he accepted the expression “of two natures,” but not that there were two natures.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But the most holy archbishop Leo says that there are two natures in Christ unchangeably, inseparably, unconfusedly united in the one only-begotten Son our Saviour.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Which would you follow, the most holy Leo or Dioscorus?

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The most reverend bishops cried out: We believe as Leo.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Leo hath rightly expounded the faith.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The most magnificent and glorious judges said: Add then to the definition, according to the judgment of our most holy father Leo, that there are two natures in Christ united unchangeably, inseparably, unconfusedly.
